description: A traditional practice or usual way of acting; what communities do by habit and agreement. 'Keno' names the worn path—the way that feels natural because it has been walked so long.
sound_symbolism: The word keeps with habit. 'Ke' holds with keeping pattern, custom maintained. 'No' settles with established ground, way accepted.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo keno' (the custom). Part of 'moenu' (culture). Related to 'thole' (practice) as habitual to intentional.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Custom as wisdom—traditional ways encoding generations of learning.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Custom as agreement—shared practices that enable smooth social interaction.
